what: Run Multiple Scenarios of the Daedalus Model
title: Run Multiple Scenarios of the Daedalus Model
text: daedalus.compare helper package wraps daedalus package epi-macroeconomic modelling, primarily focused making easy run multiple scenarios pandemic mitigation strategies along uncertainty infection parameters.

title: Run Multiple Scenarios of the Daedalus Model
text: can install development version daedalus.compare Jameel Institute R-universe (recommended) GitHub. can also install
code: # installation from R-universe install.packages(   "daedalus.compare",    repos = c(     "https://jameel-institute.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org"   ) )  # installation from GitHub using {pak} install.packages("pak") pak::pak("jameel-institute/daedalus.compare")

title: Run Multiple Scenarios of the Daedalus Model
text: example shows model multiple pandemic response scenarios U.K. uncertainty \(R_0\) H1N1-like infection. Note daedalus.compare supports running daedalus::daedalus_multi_infection() using daedalus.compare::run_scenarios() present.
code: library(daedalus) library(daedalus.compare)  # make list of infection objects with R0 of 1.0 -- 2.0 with skewed distribution set.seed(1) # for reproducible results infection_list <- make_infection_samples(   "influenza_2009",   param_distributions = list(     r0 = distributional::dist_beta(2, 5)   ),   param_ranges = list(     r0 = c(1.0, 2.0)   ),   samples = 10 )  elimination <- daedalus_timed_npi(   start_time = 10,   end_time = 100,   openness = list(     daedalus.data::closure_strategy_data[["elimination"]]   ), # must be a list   "GBR" # must specify country )  # run multiple scenarios of outputs output <- run_scenarios(   "GBR", infection_list,   response = list(     none = NULL,     elimination = elimination   ),   time_end = 200 )  # view output which is a data.table output #>       response time_end     output #>         <char>    <num>     <list> #> 1:        none      200 <list[10]> #> 2: elimination      200 <list[10]>  # get epi-curve data disease_tags <- sprintf("sample_%i", seq_along(infection_list)) epi_curves <- get_epicurve_data(output, disease_tags) # plot epi-curve data showing daily hospitalisations library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(ggplot2)  epi_curves %>%   filter(measure == "daily_hospitalisations") %>%   ggplot(aes(time, value)) +   geom_line(     aes(col = response, group = interaction(tag, response))   ) +   labs(     x = "Time", y = "Daily hospitalisations",     col = "Mitigation strategy"   )

title: Run Multiple Scenarios of the Daedalus Model
text: daedalus.compare intended helper package macro-economic epidemiological modelling package daedalus, also developed Jameel Institute. DAEDALUS model originally developed Haw et al. (2022), R implementation model based project economics pandemic preparedness.

title: Generate multiple <daedalus_infection>s from parameter distributions — make_infection_samples
code: make_infection_samples(   name,   param_distributions,   param_ranges = NULL,   samples = 100 )

title: Generate multiple <daedalus_infection>s from parameter distributions — make_infection_samples
text: name infection name among daedalus.data::epidemic_names. param_distributions named list <distribution> class objects provided distributional, names corresponding daedalus.data::infection_parameter_names. used generate samples draws distribution corresponding infection parameters. Arguments vary age supported, drawn scaled value treated median profile vector. See Examples. param_ranges optional named list two-element vectors, giving ranges samples drawn using param_distributions rescaled. allows easy rescaling values drawn bounded distributions (e.g. Beta distribution). passed, list must names corresponding param_distribution names. empty, generated values used without scaling. See Examples. samples number samples generate.

title: Generate multiple <daedalus_infection>s from parameter distributions — make_infection_samples
text: list <daedalus_infection> objects.

title: Generate multiple <daedalus_infection>s from parameter distributions — make_infection_samples
code: # make 10 infection objects varying in R0, with a skewed distribution # scaled between 1.0 and 2.0 make_infection_samples(   "influenza_2009",   samples = 3,   list(     r0 = distributional::dist_beta(2, 5)   ),   list(     r0 = c(0.1, 0.2)   ) ) #> [[1]] #> <daedalus_infection> #> • Epidemic name: influenza_2009 #> • R0: 0.119453588227766 #> • sigma: 0.909 #> • p_sigma: 0.669 #> • epsilon: 0.58 #> • rho: 0.003 #> • eta: 0.003, 0.003, 0.001, and 0.006 #> • hfr: 0.04, 0.063, 0.041, and 0.634 #> • gamma_Ia: 0.4 #> • gamma_Is: 0.4 #> • gamma_H_recovery: 0.2 #> • gamma_H_death: 0.2 #>  #> [[2]] #> <daedalus_infection> #> • Epidemic name: influenza_2009 #> • R0: 0.2 #> • sigma: 0.909 #> • p_sigma: 0.669 #> • epsilon: 0.58 #> • rho: 0.003 #> • eta: 0.003, 0.003, 0.001, and 0.006 #> • hfr: 0.04, 0.063, 0.041, and 0.634 #> • gamma_Ia: 0.4 #> • gamma_Is: 0.4 #> • gamma_H_recovery: 0.2 #> • gamma_H_death: 0.2 #>  #> [[3]] #> <daedalus_infection> #> • Epidemic name: influenza_2009 #> • R0: 0.1 #> • sigma: 0.909 #> • p_sigma: 0.669 #> • epsilon: 0.58 #> • rho: 0.003 #> • eta: 0.003, 0.003, 0.001, and 0.006 #> • hfr: 0.04, 0.063, 0.041, and 0.634 #> • gamma_Ia: 0.4 #> • gamma_Is: 0.4 #> • gamma_H_recovery: 0.2 #> • gamma_H_death: 0.2 #>

title: Run multiple DAEDALUS scenarios — run_scenarios
text: Run multiple DAEDALUS scenarios

title: Run multiple DAEDALUS scenarios — run_scenarios
code: run_scenarios(   country,   infection,   response_strategy = NULL,   vaccination_strategy = NULL,   time_end = 100,   initial_state_manual = NULL )

title: Run multiple DAEDALUS scenarios — run_scenarios
text: country country territory object class <daedalus_country>, country territory name included package; see daedalus.data::country_names, country ISO2 ISO3 code; see daedalus.data::country_codes_iso2c daedalus.data::country_codes_iso3c. Country-specific data community workplace contacts, demography, distribution workforce economic sectors automatically accessed package data relevant country name passed string. override package defaults country characteristics, pass <daedalus_country> object instead. See daedalus_country() . infection list <daedalus_infection> objects. Must minimum length 2. response_strategy time-limited response strategy specified single <daedalus_npi> created using daedalus::daedalus_timed_npi(), list objects, NULL response. Lists may include NULL, useful comparing response scenarios -response counterfactual. list passed, elements must named, list may names, case synthetic names assigned message printed screen. Lists elements named unnamed accepted. vaccination_strategy vaccination strategy specified single <daedalus_vaccination> created using daedalus::daedalus_vaccination(), list objects, NULL response. Lists may include NULL, useful comparing vaccination scenarios -vaccination counterfactual. list passed, elements must named, list may names, case synthetic names assigned message printed screen. Lists elements named unnamed accepted. time_end vector integer-ish numbers giving durations run scenarios. scenario run time_end. intention able run response scenarios different durations, able generate time-series different costs accumulate. initial_state_manual optional named list names p_infectious, p_asymptomatic, p_immune. p_infectious p_asymptomatic give proportion infectious symptomatic individuals age group economic sector. Defaults 1e-6 0.0 respectively. p_immune may single number range 0.0 <= p_immune <= 1.0 4-element vector range (number age groups model), proportion individuals population age group pre-existing immunity infection (reduced susceptibility). See Details .

title: Run multiple DAEDALUS scenarios — run_scenarios
text: <data.table>, data held list-columns.

title: daedalus.compare 0.0.6
text: patch version updates daedalus.compare compatible daedalus >= v0.2.38 introduced separate hospitalisation compartments daedalus PR 109. fixed v0.0.4. function get_epidata_list() now accesses compartments “hospitalised_recov” “hospitalised_death” calculate total hospital demand.

title: daedalus.compare 0.0.5
text: patch version allows users run multiple vaccination scenarios passing list <daedalus_vaccination> objects run_scenarios(). reorganisation data processing functions handle scenarios vary across vaccination strategies; Reorganised function documentation.

title: daedalus.compare 0.0.4
text: patch version updates {daedalus.compare} work {daedalus} v0.3.0.

title: daedalus.compare 0.0.4
text: run_scenarios() accepts <daedalus_npi>, list <daedalus_npi> NULL inputs argument response_strategy(); addition, NPIs must time-limited reactive NPIs allowed.

title: daedalus.compare 0.0.4
text: Tests documentation changed reflect change accepted NPI input types. helper function check NPI input acceptable.

title: daedalus.compare 0.0.3
text: Restores ability get epidemic summary disaggregated age groups passing arguments daedalus::get_epidemic_summary() via ... get_summary_data().

title: daedalus.compare 0.0.2
text: Updates compatibility daedalus >= v0.2.16 re-introduces list-infection inputs. Input checking run_scenarios() removed rely errors bubbled daedalus functions.

title: daedalus.compare 0.0.1
text: first version daedalus.compare adds: scenario-runner function run_scenarios() wraps around daedalus::daedalus_rtm() allows running multiple scenarios pandemic mitigation responses uncertainty infection parameters. Multiple functions downstream run_scenarios() help get data scenarios formats suitable plotting summarisation: get_epicurve_data(), get_summary_data(), get_cost_data(), get_econ_cost_data(). Helper functions constants: ci(), make_infection_samples(), NAMES_VECTOR_INF_PARAMS. Basic tests function documentation. project now includes NEWS.md file inform users changes new features.
